LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PANCHO VILLA LICENSING LLC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED AS TO THE STIPULATED ITEMS, AS WELL AS TO PROHIBIT USE OF THE NAME "TOFU RANCHERO" ON DEFENDANT'S MENU, BUT OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS AS TO THOSE ITEMS, AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES WEIGHS AGAINST THAT RELIEF. PLAINTIFF IS TO POST AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PANCHO VILLA LICENSING LLC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE.
APPEARANCES: Robert F. Knox, Esq. of Law Office of Robert F. Knox 388-9090 for pltf & moving party; Richard M. Kelly, Esq. of Kelly Litigation Group 650/591-2282 for defts & opposing parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION GRANTED AS TO THE STIPULATED ITEMS, as well as TO PROHIBIT USE OF THE NAME "TOFU RANCHERO" ON DEFENDANT'S MENU, BUT OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS AS TO THOSE ITEMS, AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES WEIGHS AGAINST THAT RELIEF. PLAINTIFF IS TO POST AN UNDERTAKING IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000. Order signed in open court. (302/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
